nodes.name: 甘肃省
nodes.summary: 甘肃省是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联9种鸟类，包括朱鹮、绿尾虹雉、点斑林鸽等9种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 稀树草原
nodes.summary: 东非常见的开放草地与湿地边缘复合景观。

nodes.name: 开阔山地
nodes.summary: 地形开阔且便于大翼展鸟类活动的山地区域。

nodes.name: EN
nodes.summary: EN表示 IUCN 濒危等级，当前关联384种鸟类，如朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭、黑脸琵鹭等384种鸟类。

nodes.name: 满洲 (地名)

nodes.summary: 为宠物或展示用途进行非法捕捉与交易。

nodes.name: 南亚
nodes.summary: 南亚是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联36种鸟类，包括斑头雁、原雞、蓝胸鹑等36种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 草原雕
nodes.summary: 草原雕（Steppe eagle，学名 Aquila nipalensis），在图谱中标记为濒危（EN）物种，已关联14个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括开阔山地、稀树草原、滩涂等4项，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏、非法猎捕、非法贸易。

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: 天山山脉

nodes.name: 东非
nodes.summary: 东非是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联42种鸟类，包括Eastern crested guinea fowl、Dusky turtle dove、Quail-plover等42种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 湿地
nodes.summary: 浅水、沼泽与植被斑块组成的高生产力生态系统。

links.evidence: The steppe eagle also breeds in large stretches of western and northern China such as Tian Shan, Xinjiang, the Gobi area, Gansu, Ningxia, northern Tibet (by far their southernmost breeding area), Inner Mongolia and reach their eastern breeding limits in Manchuria and elsewhere in northeastern China

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The steppe eagle is entirely migratory, wintering in east and, to a lesser extent, southern Africa

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The steppe eagle is entirely migratory, wintering in east and, to a lesser extent, southern Africa

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Steppe eagles predominantly use two main migration routes: one radiates across the Middle East and Arabia, with many birds stopping to winter, but many too migrate around the Red Sea to winter in Africa while the other main migration path frequently involves farther eastern breeding eagles moving along many ridges and prominent flyways before radiated across a broad path through the Himalayas, in order to reach the south Asian and other Asian wintering sites

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: 23–27). One young steppe eagle that was banded in passage in the United Arab Emirates wintered initially in Yemen before returning for the summer to Kazakhstan, then migrating to eastern Africa the following winter, showing that they can change their migratory habits over time. Many studies corroborate that steppe eagles generally migrate lesser distances as they age.Brooke, R

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: 15 birds tracked in this study were found to have migrated most frequently to winter in south Pakistan (right along the borderlands to India) or in eastern Turkmenistan

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: Wassink. Associated habitats are frequented when breeding such as flat plains, arid grassland, semi-desert and even desert edge. Most members of the species breed at lower levels but largely in eastern part of the range also will nest in poorly vegetated dry rocky hillsides such as granite massifs and upland valleys, though generally avoid truly mountainous areas

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: These include landfills and livestock carcass dumps, these being used frequently everywhere from Arabia to India. More natural habitats used most often by wintering steppe eagles tend to be various wetlands or other waterways where they are available. In winter, mostly savanna and grasslands are the predominant habitat used in Africa, also sometimes dry woodland. Study in Botswana indicated that wintering steppe eagles there appeared to be indifferent to land use changes by humans.Herremans, M., & Herremans-Tonnoeyr, D

links.label: 保护等级
links.evidence: status = EN

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Raptor Research, (26). In the borderlands of Russia and Kazakhstan, an estimated 11.9% population decline was detected in merely 6 years of study. Primary global threats appear to be habitat loss, persecution, wildfires, predation (and trampling by cattle) of chicks and electrocutions and wire collisions, especially the latter causes. Furthermore, the steppe eagles genetic diversity may be rapidly declining as well.Zinevich, L.S., Shchepetov, D.M., Sorokina, S

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Sochi. The diagnosed causes of decline in Xinjiang, Tibet and Qinghai were found mostly to be poaching, poisoning from rodent control programs (with systemic efforts dating back at least 60 years), poisoning also targeted towards predators, illegal trade, food shortages and wire collisions but perhaps most of all habitat destruction, often with their former homes destroyed to make way for roadworks, tourism and mine exploration, with more destruction from overharvest of trees and plants and overgrazing by livestock, and accidental are frequent. Poisoning is thought to be quite prevalent in the Altais, as well as powerline electrocutions. A mean of 13.3 individual steppe eagles in Kazakhstan were estimated to be killed by each of powerline.Berezovikov, N.N
